source.description: One reviewed six-panel raster contact sheet, cropped into five process masters and the final lighthouse master.
source.notes: Lock the three-quarter shore-side view, lighthouse proportions, and left-to-right beam direction across every panel.

process_strategy: A single six-panel pencil process keeps the same lighthouse on its rocky point; each frame adds only the next structural layer so the last panel only deepens existing graphite and light color accents.

final_elements.name: lighthouse tower
final_elements.description: A tall tapered tower with a gently curved base, centered slightly left.

final_elements.name: lantern room and roof
final_elements.description: A small windowed lantern room beneath a shallow cap roof.

final_elements.name: rocky point
final_elements.description: Two layered rock ledges anchoring the tower along the bottom edge.

final_elements.name: shore wave bands
final_elements.description: Three shallow, curving water lines crossing the foreground.

final_elements.name: tower stripes and window panes
final_elements.description: Two horizontal stripe bands and simple dark lantern-window dividers.

final_elements.name: cloud and light beam
final_elements.description: A small soft cloud and a narrow pale beam pointing toward the upper right.

frames.visible_job: Lay in a light center axis, tapered tower silhouette, and the two rock ledges.
Set the roof ellipse and small lantern-room box on the already-established tower.
Add three foreground wave bands that tuck behind the established rocky point.
Add the tower stripe bands and lantern-window dividers to existing forms.
Place the small cloud and narrow beam after the roof direction is clear.

final_step.visible_job: Strengthen the existing pencil contours and add restrained blue-gray water and warm beam shading only to established shapes.

rejection_checks: Reject if the tower, lantern room, rock silhouette, beam, or viewpoint changes between panels.
Reject if a rock, roof, window, stripe, wave, cloud, or beam first appears in the final panel.
Reject if the finish looks like polished vector art rather than handmade pencil on warm paper.
